title: Channels
subtitle: App, web, contact center, employee copilot
whatItDoes: Receives the user's request across customer, advisor, and employee touchpoints while preserving a consistent interaction model.
whyItMatters: The same trusted answer experience should work whether the request starts in a mobile app, service portal, or assisted channel.
howItScales: Channel adapters normalize requests so the platform can serve millions of sessions without building separate AI stacks for every surface.
architectFocus: Design shared identity, session handoff, and accessibility patterns across channels.

controls.label: Session continuity
controls.hint: Carry context between digital self-service and human-assisted journeys.

controls.label: Accessibility
controls.hint: Support keyboard, voice, and clear answer presentation from the first release.

title: Experience layer
subtitle: Chat UI, voice input, prompt suggestions
whatItDoes: Shapes the answer engine experience through prompts, voice and text input, trust signals, and concise response presentation.
whyItMatters: Users judge the AI by how quickly it feels helpful and trustworthy, not by the elegance of the backend.
howItScales: A shared experience layer lets teams evolve prompts, answer layouts, and trust patterns without rewriting platform logic.
architectFocus: Define interaction standards that make citations, controls, and follow-up discovery feel native.

controls.label: Prompt UX
controls.hint: Curate suggested prompts so the assistant feels guided instead of open-ended.

controls.label: Trust surfaces
controls.hint: Expose sources and confidence without overwhelming the customer.

title: AI orchestration layer
subtitle: Prompt routing, session memory, tool execution
whatItDoes: Routes requests to the right answer path, maintains useful context, and coordinates retrieval, tools, and answer synthesis.
whyItMatters: This is where a basic chat interface becomes an enterprise answer engine with structured decision logic.
howItScales: Orchestration enables reusable answer patterns, safer tool use, and consistent behavior across many products and tenants.
architectFocus: Design routing policies, scoped memory, and execution boundaries that balance speed with control.

controls.label: Intent routing
controls.hint: Choose between public-answer, enterprise-grounded, and escalation flows.

controls.label: Memory scope
controls.hint: Persist only the context that improves the session safely.

title: Retrieval layer
subtitle: Search, vector retrieval, policy-aware grounding
whatItDoes: Finds the most relevant approved information and applies permissions, freshness, and policy checks before the model uses it.
whyItMatters: Strong retrieval is the difference between plausible language and enterprise-grade answers.
howItScales: The retrieval layer supports multiple knowledge domains, access rules, and search strategies without changing the front-end experience.
architectFocus: Define search quality metrics, permission models, and freshness rules for every source domain.

controls.label: Freshness policy
controls.hint: Prioritize current, approved content and block stale knowledge in sensitive flows.

controls.label: Permission checks
controls.hint: Apply source-level access controls before documents reach the model.

title: Source layer
subtitle: Web, knowledge base, contracts, service docs, product catalogs
whatItDoes: Holds the public and enterprise content that the answer engine can cite and rely on.
whyItMatters: Architects need to know which content is authoritative, how it changes, and how trustworthy answers stay current.
howItScales: A governed source layer lets new domains be added quickly without losing provenance or compliance boundaries.
architectFocus: Establish content ownership, ingestion pipelines, and evidence quality standards.

controls.label: Content ownership
controls.hint: Every source domain needs a named owner for quality and policy alignment.

controls.label: Evidence quality
controls.hint: Track completeness, freshness, and citation usefulness over time.

title: Model gateway
subtitle: Model routing, fallback logic, cost controls
whatItDoes: Selects the right model for each request while enforcing regional, cost, and resilience policies.
whyItMatters: Enterprise platforms need model flexibility without exposing product teams directly to every model-specific risk.
howItScales: A gateway decouples the experience from model churn, enabling failover, experimentation, and cost-aware routing at platform level.
architectFocus: Define routing criteria, fallback policies, and approved model envelopes by workload.

controls.label: Fallback policy
controls.hint: Choose what happens when a preferred model fails latency, cost, or policy checks.

controls.label: Cost budget
controls.hint: Apply per-intent cost controls so scale does not erode business value.

title: Governance layer
subtitle: Guardrails, compliance, human approval, redaction
whatItDoes: Applies inline controls for privacy, safety, compliance, escalation, and traceability before an answer is delivered or acted on.
whyItMatters: Trust has to be visible in the product, but enforceable in the platform.
howItScales: Inline governance lets organizations handle millions of requests without depending on manual review for ordinary cases.
architectFocus: Turn policy intent into executable controls that teams can audit and evolve.

controls.label: Human approval
controls.hint: Escalate sensitive or low-confidence outcomes into reviewable workflows.

controls.label: Redaction
controls.hint: Protect PII and regulated data before prompts, logs, or outputs are stored.

title: Operations layer
subtitle: Monitoring, evals, feedback, A/B testing, incident handling
whatItDoes: Measures quality, reliability, adoption, and safety so the platform can improve continuously and recover safely when things drift.
whyItMatters: Without operating discipline, even a good pilot will break under scale or changing user behavior.
howItScales: Shared observability, evaluation, and rollout controls let teams ship improvements without losing confidence.
architectFocus: Define which signals matter, how experiments are governed, and what rollback looks like.

controls.label: Evaluation loop
controls.hint: Continuously test groundedness, safety, and helpfulness against target workloads.

controls.label: Canary release
controls.hint: Expose changes to controlled traffic before broad rollout.

title: Infrastructure layer
subtitle: Sovereign cloud, multi-region, tenant-aware deployment
whatItDoes: Provides the runtime, network, data, and isolation foundations that keep the platform reliable and regionally compliant.
whyItMatters: This is where enterprise requirements such as sovereignty, resilience, and tenant isolation become real architecture decisions.
howItScales: Infrastructure patterns determine whether the service can absorb burst traffic, regional failover, and regulated workloads safely.
architectFocus: Plan region strategy, workload isolation, resilience targets, and deployment automation.

controls.label: Tenant isolation
controls.hint: Keep customer data, prompts, and retrieval paths isolated across enterprise tenants.

controls.label: Regional failover
controls.hint: Recover service quickly without violating residency or policy constraints.
